name: Reopen The Measured 64x384 Fixed-Main-Tile Control Path

summary.name: Reopen The Measured 64x384 Fixed-Main-Tile Control Path
summary.hypothesis: Round 12/17 closes the grouping family for now: the 4-row retry was the best member of that family on the accepted export base, but tightening further to 2 rows regressed back to 26.06694412 ms and pushed DRAM up to 21.47. That means the local PTX-adjacent grouping lever has likely plateaued. The next best move is to reopen the older but directly measured `64x384` fixed-main-tile control path, which is the strongest remaining alternate family with explicit ranking evidence in the repo.
summary.expected_bottleneck: Broader hot-band path selection and arithmetic-intensity tradeoff rather than PTX helper overhead.
summary.risk: Medium. The family has explicit sweep ancestry, but it is a broad branch change that can easily give back the wins from the accepted PTX/export base.
summary.stop_condition: Stop immediately if the 64x384 control path remains materially slower than 24.84582424 ms or clearly reopens a broad-family regression.

notes: Build passed. Node A must measure this implementation next.
